title: A Tiny Dinosaur with Long Arms Changes What We Know About Small Dinosaurs
summary: Scientists found a new tiny dinosaur called Alnashetri that lived 90 million years ago in Argentina. It was very small, weighing only 700 grams, and had long arms, unlike other small dinosaurs in its group called Alvarezsaurids. Before, people thought these dinosaurs got small to eat ants and termites, but Alnashetri probably hunted insects and small mammals instead. This discovery shows that dinosaurs didn't always get smaller in a straight line for one reason. It helps us learn that evolution can be more complicated, with body sizes changing in different ways. The fossil is important because it's the most complete and smallest of its kind found in South America.

questions.question: What is the name of the new tiny dinosaur found in Argentina?
What did scientists used to think small Alvarezsaurid dinosaurs ate?
What did Alnashetri probably hunt instead?
Why is Alnashetri important for understanding evolution?
options: It shows dinosaurs didn't always get smaller in a straight line
It proves all dinosaurs were huge
It means dinosaurs didn't evolve at all
It shows dinosaurs only lived in one place
questions.correct_answer: It shows dinosaurs didn't always get smaller in a straight line

background_read: Dinosaurs are ancient animals that lived millions of years ago, and scientists study their fossils to learn how they lived and changed over time. Some dinosaurs were very big, like T. rex, while others were small. Evolution is the process where animals adapt to their environment, sometimes getting smaller or developing new features. Alvarezsaurids are a group of small dinosaurs that scientists thought got tiny to eat ants, but new discoveries like Alnashetri show that evolution can be more complex, with different reasons for size changes.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists and paleontologists like Peter Makovicky
WHAT: Found a new tiny dinosaur called Alnashetri that changes ideas about dinosaur miniaturization
WHERE: In Argentina's Río Negro Province
WHY: To understand how dinosaurs evolved and became small, showing it wasn't just for eating ants

perspectives.description: Finding Alnashetri helps scientists rethink how dinosaurs evolved to be small, showing it's more complicated than previously thought.
